Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated creatinine, suggesting impaired renal function. Urinalysis reveals significant proteinuria, glucosuria, ketonuria, low specific gravity, and abnormal urine appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal), indicating possible nephrotic syndrome or advanced renal disease. HbA1c is low, ruling out chronic hyperglycemia. No evidence of urinary infection (negative nitrites/leukocytes). Recommend further nephrological evaluation and assessment for underlying causes of proteinuria and renal impairment.
